name: Stuffed Zucchini with Yogurt Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash zucchini well. Do not trim ends. Cut each zucchini in half lengthwise. Scoop out all seeded portion of zucchini flesh. Sprinkle a tiny amount of salt into each shell. Set aside for an hour.
2. Wash and drain lentils. Simmer them in 2 cups water for 2 minutes, then let sit, covered, for 45 minutes.
3. In a bowl, cover bulgur with 3 cups water and let sit for an hour.
4. When lentils have sat for 45 minutes, bring to simmer again. Lower heat and simmer for about 10 minutes or until tender. Drain and put in a bowl.
5. Drain bulgur and squeeze out as much liquid as possible. Put bulgur in the same bowl as lentils.
6. Heat olive oil in skillet over medium flame. Saute onion for 2 minutes. Add tomato, saute for another 2 minutes. Mix saute with lentils and bulgur. Add garlic, salt, lemon juice, and parsley, mix well.
7. Arrange a steamer by setting a colander on top of a large pot with an inch or so of water. Stuff zucchini with lentil-bulgur mixture. Place stuffed zucchini in colander and cover.
8. Steam for 10-15 minutes until zucchini shells are just tender.
9. Sauce: Mix cornstarch and 1 tablespoon water in a bowl. Add the yogurt. Whisk until creamy. Put yogurt in heavy saucepan and bring to simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly. When yogurt begins to bubble, lower heat and cook for 5 minutes, stirring gently. Add remaining sauce ingredients and mix.
10. Put cooked stuffed zucchini on plates and top with sauce.
